AGPL-3.0 Licensed

Open Source

Ralph is free software. Forever.

01

The License

What AGPL-3.0 means for you

Ralph is licensed under the GNU Affero General Public License v3.0 (AGPL-3.0). This is a strong copyleft license designed to keep software free.

In Plain English

You CAN

  • Use Ralph for any purpose
  • Modify Ralph's source code
  • Distribute Ralph (with source)
  • Use Ralph commercially
  • Run Ralph as part of a service

You MUST

  • Keep the same license (AGPL-3.0)
  • Provide source code to users
  • State your changes clearly
  • Include original copyright notice

You CANNOT

  • Make it proprietary
  • Remove the license
  • Hold us liable

Important Clarification

The AGPL license applies to Ralph itself—not to the code Ralph generates for you.

Your PROMPT.md files and the code Ralph writes for your projects are yours. Ralph is just a tool, like a compiler. The output belongs to you.

Read the Full License
02

Contributing

Help make Ralph better

Ralph is built by contributors like you. Whether you're fixing bugs, adding features, improving documentation, or sharing feedback—every contribution matters.

Report Issues

Found a bug? Something not working right? Open an issue on Codeberg with reproduction steps.

Open an Issue →

Submit Pull Requests

Fork the repo, make your changes, and submit a PR. We review all contributions.

Fork on Codeberg →

Improve Documentation

Docs are code too. Help us make Ralph easier to understand and use.

View Docs →

Share Feedback

Ideas for features? Workflow improvements? We want to hear from you.

Start a Discussion →

Development Setup

Want to hack on Ralph? Here's how to get started:

bash 
# Clone the repository
git clone ssh://git@codeberg.org/mistlight/Ralph-Workflow.git
cd Ralph-Workflow

# Build in development mode
cargo build

# Run tests
cargo test

# Run with debug output
RUST_LOG=debug cargo run -- -S
03

Project Values

What we believe in

Freedom First

Software should be free—free to use, free to modify, free to share. The AGPL ensures Ralph stays free forever.

Transparency

Open source means you can see exactly what Ralph does. No hidden behaviors, no data collection, no surprises.

User Ownership

Ralph runs on your machine, with your API keys. You're in control. No vendor lock-in, no subscription required.

Community Driven

Ralph exists because of contributors. Every bug report, PR, and feature suggestion shapes the project.

04

Acknowledgments

Standing on the shoulders of giants

Ralph is inspired by and built upon the work of many:

  • Geoffrey Huntley — The original Ralph concept and loop methodology
  • The Rust Community — For building the tools and ecosystem that make Ralph possible
  • Codeberg — For hosting open source projects without surveillance capitalism
  • All Contributors — Everyone who has reported bugs, submitted PRs, or spread the word

Join the community

Star the repo, submit a PR, or just say hello. Every contribution matters.

Star on Codeberg Get Started

Ralph Workflow

Multi-agent AI orchestration for automated code development. Ship your ideas with autonomous agents that collaborate until quality is achieved.
